diagrams.title: Basic Parse Tree Structure
diagrams.description: Shows root (start symbol S), internal nodes (non-terminals A,B), and leaf nodes (terminals a,b,c)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding hierarchical structure and node types

diagrams.title: Expression Parse Tree
diagrams.description: Parse tree for expression a + b * c showing operator precedence
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how operators become internal nodes and operands become leaves

diagrams.title: Leftmost Derivation Tree
diagrams.description: Step-by-step parse tree built via leftmost derivation for grammar S→aSbS|ε
elements: Shows expansion of leftmost non-terminal at each step
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how derivations construct parse trees

diagrams.title: Ambiguous Grammar Parse Tree
diagrams.description: Two different parse trees for same string if-then-else using grammar: S→if S then S|if S then S else S|other
diagrams.purpose: Understanding ambiguity and why it's problematic in compilers
